title: Vita nuova (Uusi elämä)
summaries: "Vita nuova (Uusi elämä)" by Dante Alighieri is a text published in 1294. This groundbreaking work combines poetry and prose to chronicle Dante's love for Beatrice from their first meeting at age nine through her death and beyond. Through twenty-five sonnets and other verse forms with autobiographical commentary, Dante transforms courtly love poetry into something sacred, using personal emotion to explore divine love. Written in Italian rather than Latin, it marked a revolutionary shift in European literature. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
